fields.remark: Here’s another outfit capturing to perfection Gianfranco Ferré’s passion for mingling masculine and feminine fashion with thoughtful elegance. The extralong jacket references the 19th century men’s tailcoat, where the new bombé styling in no way took away from the original design of this classic article of bourgeois clothing.
fields.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
fields.description: Jacket in black grain de poudre wool. High waist, amphora shape. Radial arm construction with narrowing toward lower edge of sleeve. Round tip lapels down to waist. One-button closure. Besom pockets, plus a flap pocket on left. Knee-skimming length. Black silk crepe de chine lining. Skirt in black grain de poudre wool. Four panel construction. Slim line, knee length. Shirt in white tubular silk georgette. Kimono style sleeves narrowing at slit cuff. Mannish silk satin collar in bombé cut. Hip- hugging basque for a blousy effect. Bow in brown silk taffeta. Long and wide (5 cm) with 1 cm parallel topstiching.
fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.materials: Grain de poudre wool (Schontess, Milano; art. 799, col. black), pure silk tubular georgette (Clerici Tessuto, art. 1754, des. 110), silk satin, silk taffeta, silk crepe de chine

Parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing
Jacket in black grain de poudre wool. High waist, amphora shape. Radial arm construction with narrowing toward lower edge
of sleeve. Round tip lapels down to waist. One-button closure. Besom pockets, plus a flap pocket on left. Knee-skimming
length. Black silk crepe de chine lining.
Skirt in black grain de poudre wool. Four panel construction. Slim line, knee length.
Shirt in white tubular silk georgette. Kimono style sleeves narrowing at slit cuff. Mannish silk satin collar in bombé cut. Hip-
hugging basque for a blousy effect.
Bow in brown silk taffeta. Long and wide (5 cm) with 1 cm parallel topstiching.
Here’s another outfit capturing to perfection Gianfranco Ferré’s passion for mingling masculine and feminine fashion with
thoughtful elegance. The extralong jacket references the 19th century men’s tailcoat, where the new bombé styling in no
way took away from the original design of this classic article of bourgeois clothing.
FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
28/07/2014

fields.remark: What makes this outfit elegantly unique and recherché is the synergistic relationship between feminine chic and mannish style, between fanciful dots and severe stripes.
fields.description: T-shirt in glossy brown crocodile leather. Elbow-length kimono sleeves. Crew neck with opening in back. Pants in brown/white chalkstripe wool. High waist with two tucks. Straight leg, ankle length. Belt in glossy brown crocodile leather with rectangular buckle. Bow in brown/white polkadot chiffon. Lavish proportions with two-button closure. Detachable.
fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.materials: Chalkstripe wool (Schontess), print silk chiffon (Clerici Tessuto, Como), genuine crocodile leather with high gloss finish

Belt in glossy brown crocodile leather with rectangular buckle.
Bow in brown/white polkadot chiffon. Lavish proportions with two-button closure. Detachable.
What makes this outfit elegantly unique and recherché is the synergistic relationship between feminine chic and mannish
style, between fanciful dots and severe stripes.

fields.description: Canotta di linea aderente, priva di maniche e lunga al secondo fianco, con chiusura anteriore a girocollo e profonda scollatura a V sul retro. Il completo è completamente ricoperto da paillettes sino all’alta bordura inferiore in pelliccia, che nasconde, posteriormente, una piccola chiusura a zip.
fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
fields.materials: Georgette di seta marrone, pelliccia di volpe al naturale, paillettes a stampa cangiante ad effetto tartaruga

fields.remark: This dress is clearly an expression of Gianfranco Ferré’s intrinsic passion for hyperbole, his pursuit of elements of exaggeration and excess (in reality, always calculated and calibrated to perfection)
fields.description: Dress bustier style. Form fit, knee length. With silk tape on brown organza silk lining zigzagging across the whole dress. Silk taffeta ribbon on bodice, knotting into a bow complete with big velvet pansy
fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.working_process: Extensive application of silk tape; parallel topstitching with 1 cm spacing
fields.materials: Silk organza (Clerici Tessuto, Como), 1 cm wide silk tape, silk taffeta (Clerici Tessuto, Como)

fields.remark: Some true hallmarks of the Ferré style confirm the authenticity of this creation: namely, the luxurious materials and the gorgeous details, all at once decorative and structural.
fields.description: Dress with brown/black macro houndstooth check silk skirt. Slim line, knee length, wrap style closure in back with diagonally overlapping edges. Form-fitting bustier bodice in black lurex lace with truncated cone element (richly embroidered with flowers & spirals) opening on one side to create a corolla effect. Long brown velvet ribbon with 1 cm parallel topstitching.
fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.materials: Silk jacquard (Schoentess, Milano; art. 122, des. 5181, var.1), cotton/lurex lace, silk velvet

flowers & spirals) opening on one side to create a corolla effect. Long brown velvet ribbon with 1 cm parallel topstitching.
Some true hallmarks of the Ferré style confirm the authenticity of this creation: namely, the luxurious materials and the
gorgeous details, all at once decorative and structural.

fields.remark: Here’s a 20th century take (very much in feminine version) on the 18th century young man’s redingote. Distinguishing features include the silk crêpe fabric complete with meticulous cutwork: the classic eyelet effect is the amazing result of hand craftsmanship performed on the finished garment.
fields.description: Dress redingote style in black cutwork silk crêpe with black silk satin lining. Slim bodice with little lapel collar, padded shoulders, long narrow sleeves. Front metal-clasp closure with small overlay. Skirt with lovely A-line flare. Under the collar are big black organza & velvet leaf appliqués, with matching topstitched silk satin ribbon to knot below. Black organza lining for top part of the dress. Then for added fullness, the flaring skirt has a big nylon flounce on the inside.
fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.working_process: Silk fabric all in cutwork motif
fields.materials: Silk crepe, silk satin, silk organza, cotton velvet, nylon

raw_text:
N. 469
File
Dress redingote
Gianfranco Ferré
Couture
Fall - Winter
1987
Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
Silk crepe, silk satin, silk organza, cotton velvet, nylon
Silk fabric all in cutwork motif
Dress redingote style in black cutwork silk crêpe with black silk satin lining. Slim bodice with little lapel collar, padded
shoulders, long narrow sleeves. Front metal-clasp closure with small overlay. Skirt with lovely A-line flare. Under the collar
are big black organza & velvet leaf appliqués, with matching topstitched silk satin ribbon to knot below. Black organza lining
for top part of the dress. Then for added fullness, the flaring skirt has a big nylon flounce on the inside.
Here’s a 20th century take (very much in feminine version) on the 18th century young man’s redingote. Distinguishing
features include the silk crêpe fabric complete with meticulous cutwork: the classic eyelet effect is the amazing result of hand
craftsmanship performed on the finished garment.
28/07/2014

fields.remark: While an exquisite expression of severity elegance, this dress holds elements of surprise: the sumptuous decor at right wrist level and the big bow at back waist (totally invisible from the front).
fields.description: Coat-dress in black silk marocain. Six-part construction, amphora line, ankle length. Kimono style sleeves narrowing toward slit lower edge (to turn up in a cuff). Bombé-cut lapels & mid bottom opening. Black crepe de chine lining. Two black silk taffeta ribbons (20 cm wide) with 1 cm parallel topstitching at sides of upper back knot into a bow.
fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.materials: Silk marocain (Taroni, Como; art. 17028), silk taffeta (Taroni, Como; art. 2829, col. black

fields.remark: This dress captures an at once perfect and singular balance of shapes, volumes, proportions: an amazing sum of parts in sublime opposition to one another.
fields.description: Dress in brown silk taffeta with decorative topstitching. Slim strapless bodice in double layer of silk faille (30 cm wide ribbon) crisscrossing neatly in front and then knotting obi style in back. Under the bustline a circular panel of topstitched silk taffeta flares beautifully outward from large fluid pleats.
fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.working_process: 1 cm. parallel topstitching all along the circular panel
fields.materials: Silk taffeta (Taroni, Como; art. 3057, var. 824), silk faille (Clerici Tessuto; Como), nylon

taffeta flares beautifully outward from large fluid pleats.
This dress captures an at once perfect and singular balance of shapes, volumes, proportions: an amazing sum of parts in
sublime opposition to one another.

fields.remark: Tutto si arrotonda, tutto si dilata. Il capo ondeggia nella sua ruota quasi conica con un movimento fermato nel tempo, in perfetto equilibrio di volumi e proporzioni.
fields.acquisition: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
fields.description: Abito – bustier composto da più elementi che si sovrappongono: il bustino è formato da due tronchi di cono che si aprono a corolla sul davanti; la parte inferiore è costituita da una gonna a ¾ di ruota su cui è cucito un elemento a mezza ruota a formare pieghe. La crinolina di nylon inserita nei profili esterni sostiene le ampiezze e favorisce un effetto plastico e tridimensionale.
fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (scritta oro)
fields.working_process: impunture parallele a mm10 seguono l’andamento delle forme geometriche del capo
fields.materials: Taffeta di seta (Clerici Tessuto, Como art. 0802 - col. nero) trapunto e matelassé; duchesse di seta (Clerici Tessuto, Como); crinolina di nylon.

impunture parallele a mm10 seguono l’andamento delle forme geometriche del capo
Abito – bustier composto da più elementi che si sovrappongono: il bustino è formato da due tronchi di cono che si aprono a
corolla sul davanti; la parte inferiore è costituita da una gonna a ¾ di ruota su cui è cucito un elemento a mezza ruota a
formare pieghe. La crinolina di nylon inserita nei profili esterni sostiene le ampiezze e favorisce un effetto plastico e
tridimensionale.
Tutto si arrotonda, tutto si dilata. Il capo ondeggia nella sua ruota quasi conica con un movimento fermato nel tempo, in
perfetto equilibrio di volumi e proporzioni.
Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano

fields.remark: The outstanding feature here is the artful exaggeration of volumes and proportions, so revealing once again the designer’s love of hyperbole. As for a totally new effect, it’s there in how in back the large skirt rests on the short slim one underneath simply by making the most of a natural drape.
fields.description: Dress ballgown style in silk faille with giant stripes (black, white, fuchsia, silver). Slim strapless bodice with low-cut V neckline in back. Voluminous skirt thanks to large fluid pleats. Brown silk floral & spiral embroidery on wide black band at hip level. Black silk gazar lining. Skirt (underneath) in brown silk velvet with 1 cm topstitching. Mid-thigh length. Knotted black silk faille ribbon on waistband.
fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.working_process: Complex brown silk relief embroidery; parallel topstiching with 1 cm spacing
fields.materials: Silk faille in wide bands of color (Clerici Tessuto, Como; des. 2, var. 2), silk velvet (Stucchi, Como), silk velvet (Stucchi, Como; col. brown)

Dress ballgown style in silk faille with giant stripes (black, white, fuchsia, silver). Slim strapless bodice with low-cut V
neckline in back. Voluminous skirt thanks to large fluid pleats. Brown silk floral & spiral embroidery on wide black band at hip
level. Black silk gazar lining.
Skirt (underneath) in brown silk velvet with 1 cm topstitching. Mid-thigh length. Knotted black silk faille ribbon on
waistband.
The outstanding feature here is the artful exaggeration of volumes and proportions, so revealing once again the designer’s
love of hyperbole. As for a totally new effect, it’s there in how in back the large skirt rests on the short slim one underneath
simply by making the most of a natural drape.
28/07/2014

fields.remark: Again, the outstanding feature is an artful exaggeration of volumes and proportions revealing the designer’s love of hyperbole. As for a totally new effect, it’s there in how the long full skirt rests on the short one underneath simply by making the most of a natural drape. With additional support from the big satiny silk bow.
fields.present_location: Spazio FGF c/o Open Care - Milano
fields.description: Dress with a bustier made of white cotton honeycomb piqué and a skirt in purple silk faille with black and gold stripes, with a satin weave that does not appear on the reverse side.The panels are assembled with large loose pleats in the skirt and are hollowed out from the waist up. Black silk organza lining. A topstitched satin ribbon at 0.5 cm is attached to the dress, tied in a bow at the front, over which the skirt drapes to form a drape. Nylon crinoline structure supports the shape and weight of the skirt. Brown velvet underskirt; fitted; mid-thigh length.
fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.author_of_file:
Vidotto / Airaghi
Ilaria Trame
Margherita Rosina
fields.materials: White piqué; purple silk faille with black and gold stripes (Clerici Tessuto, Como; design 3 var.4); brown silk velvet (Stucchi, Como); black silk organza (Clerici Tessuto, Como); nylon crinoline.

White piqué; purple silk faille with black and gold stripes (Clerici Tessuto, Como; design 3 var.4); brown silk velvet (Stucchi,
Como); black silk organza (Clerici Tessuto, Como); nylon crinoline.
Parallel topstitching at 0.5 cm, embroidery.
Dress with a bustier made of white cotton honeycomb piqué and a skirt in purple silk faille with black and gold stripes, with
a satin weave that does not appear on the reverse side.The panels are assembled with large loose pleats in the skirt and are
hollowed out from the waist up. Black silk organza lining. A topstitched satin ribbon at 0.5 cm is attached to the dress, tied in
a bow at the front, over which the skirt drapes to form a drape. Nylon crinoline structure supports the shape and weight of
the skirt.
Brown velvet underskirt; fitted; mid-thigh length.
Again, the outstanding feature is an artful exaggeration of volumes and proportions revealing the designer’s love of
hyperbole. As for a totally new effect, it’s there in how the long full skirt rests on the short one underneath simply by making
the most of a natural drape. With additional support from the big satiny silk bow.

fields.remark: A dark lady of past times. Here the chaste severity of the Victorian Age is offset by quiet elements of wicked femininity, as in the gold flounces with gilt sequins peeking out from under the skirt’s asymmetric hemline.
fields.description: Dress in black wool crepe with eight-part construction. Long lavishly flaring skirt with asymmetric front hem. Underneath, black nylon crinoline petticoat with three gold lamé plisse flounces. Long bodice with eight-part construction. Round neck, narrow wrist-length sleeves.
fields.label: Gianfranco Ferré couture (gold lettering)
fields.working_process: Sunburst pleating on petticoat flounces; sequining
fields.materials: Wool crepe (Schontess, Milano), black nylon, gold lurex lace, gold sequins

fields.remark: The dark lady is back, in strapless version with long elegant evening gloves and again the glimpse of gold plisse flounces. All marks of an exquisitely enticing femininity.
fields.present_location: FGF space c/o Open Care - Milan
fields.description: Dress in dark wine red silk velvet. Strapless bodice with topstitching. Long lavishly flaring skirt with three-part construction. Two shorter sections in front, one longer one in back with hint of a train. Black nylon crinoline petticoat with three gold lamé plisse flounces peeking out from under the skirt’s asymmetric hemline.
